content: In this lecture, Ian Ball explains bargaining with incomplete information, a scenario in which at least one party lacks full knowledge of the other’s private information. Plea bargains are an example of this.
title: Lecture 23: Bargaining with Incomplete Information

video_metadata.video_tags: 14-12-economic-applications-of-game-theory-fall-2025, bargaining, incomplete information, one-side, plea bargain, screening, signaling, two-sided
video_metadata.youtube_description:
MIT 14.12 Economic Applications of Game Theory, Fall 2025 
Instructor: Ian Ball 
View the complete course: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/14-12-economic-applications-of-game-theory-fall-2025/
YouTube Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLUl4u3cNGP63quuKvMHCt3cmTmt0O2qpv

In this lecture, Ian Ball explains bargaining with incomplete information, which is a scenario where at least one party lacks full knowledge of the other's private information. Plea bargains are an example of this.
